label: UI Keyed Contract Sheet (steel revision)
description: REVISION -- overwrites the original gold-palette sheet. Kirk's art-direction verdict on the original commit: 'gold is a bit much, I like the steel or dark steel look.' Every dnd5e:ui:* key grouped by surface (9-slice chrome, bar/gauge fills, status badges, initiative portraits, victory/defeat banners). frame-panel/frame-ornate/frame-button now use silver/steel/chrome sprites (previously gold); bar-fill-hp/bar-fill-resource swapped roles (silver now HP, gold now the secondary-resource accent -- the pack's only 2 solid bar fills, no red/crimson exists); banner-victory now uses a bright silver/chrome celebratory-shaped banner (previously warm gold scrollwork) -- banner-defeat (cold steel) is unchanged. 9-slice chrome entries are shown stretched BOTH 3x wide and 3x tall (widened from wide-only) to verify sliceInsets don't distort corners on either axis. See rpg-game-assets#18 and library/ui-contract-key-map.json's $steelRevisionNote for the full pick-by-pick rationale, including a rejected candidate (Frame_Box20) whose off-center ornament distorted under stretch.
supersedes: pre-revision commit on rpg-game-assets#18 (gold-palette contract sheet, gate-reviewed prior to this revision)

label: Generated contract manifest (steel revision)
description: Copy of harness/models/synty/ui/contract/manifest.json from rpg-game-assets#18 at the time this evidence was captured -- generated by scripts/build_ui_contract_manifest.py from library/ui-contract-key-map.json. Reflects the steel-revision picks (see contract-sheet.png's description above); a re-gate of this manifest will follow.
